use tree_sitter::Parser;

fn parse_files(files: &[&str]) {
    for file_path in files {
        let source = std::fs::read_to_string(file_path).unwrap();
        let mut parser = Parser::new();
        let language = get_language_for_file(file_path);
        parser.set_language(&language.into()).unwrap();
        let tree = parser.parse(&source, None).unwrap();
        process_tree(tree.root_node(), &source);
    }
}

fn get_language_for_file(path: &str) -> tree_sitter::Language {
    if path.ends_with(".rs") {
        tree_sitter_rust::LANGUAGE.into()
    } else if path.ends_with(".py") {
        tree_sitter_python::LANGUAGE.into()
    } else {
        tree_sitter_rust::LANGUAGE.into()
    }
}

fn process_tree(_node: tree_sitter::Node, _source: &str) {}
